verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich secretory protein with an N-terminal signal peptide but no transmembrane domain. It is expressed and secreted in the epididymis, where it binds non-covalently to the sperm surface as part of sperm maturation and fertilization. The protein body itself lacks any intrinsic membrane anchor (no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no lipidation). It associates with sperm through reversible, non-covalent binding and can be released during capacitation, placing it in equilibrium with the soluble pool. The protein is the ligand recruiter, not the surface target. The sperm is the cell with surface anchors; CRISP1 itself is secreted-only.
